title: premna angolensis
intro: Premna angolensis, also known as African tea tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae and is native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, such as fever, headaches, and respiratory problems, and have also been shown to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved cardiovascular health. Premna angolensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it easily accessible for herbal remedies.
